expected_signals.summary: Presents a continuous-query limited memory language model design in which the knowledge base uses continuous keys and textual knowledge values.
expected_signals.anchor_quote: pairs continuous keys with textual knowledge values

expected_signals.summary: Adds an annotation pipeline for tagging free-form factual spans in arbitrary text, removing the prior restriction to Wikipedia.
expected_signals.anchor_quote: removing prior work's restriction to Wikipedia

expected_signals.summary: Claims Co-LMLM outperforms prior limited-memory language models and vanilla LLMs in perplexity and factual precision across multiple scales.
expected_signals.anchor_quote: outperforms prior LMLMs and vanilla LLMs

expected_signals.summary: At 360M scale, reports lower perplexity than models pretrained on 40 times more data.
Claims SimpleQA-verified performance is in line with gpt-4o-mini and higher than Claude Sonnet 4.5.
expected_signals.anchor_quote: in line with gpt-4o-mini and higher than Claude Sonnet 4.5

notes: Observable proof for this labeling task is limited to source-text grounding: each anchor_quote is a verbatim substring from the provided abstract. The method name appears as Co-LMLM in the title and CO-LMLM in the abstract.
